summary: Sharp matrix recording captures a high‑energy Philly show highlighted by a smoking Women > Eyes, monster Other One and crisp Box of Rain.
review: The matrix source delivers excellent clarity with just enough audience ambience to convey the night’s electric vibe.  The performance is buoyant – the second set erupts on Women > Eyes and rolls into an extended, searing Other One, while the first set builds momentum with a standout Box of Rain and solid Never Trust A Woman.  A few reviewers note a sluggish start (Jack Straw) and a slightly froggy Jerry vocal, but overall the show is remembered as one of the band’s best runs in Philadelphia.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews, most 4‑5 stars, uniformly praise the sound and highlight multiple standout songs; only a handful of mild criticisms keep the rating just below “exceptional.”

recording_quality.technical_notes: Sharp, well‑balanced mix with clear instrument separation; occasional audience bleed noted but never detracts from overall fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds steadily but is described by a few as slightly sluggish; the second set ignites with Women > Eyes and sustains high energy through a monster Other One, showcasing superb transitions and crowd‑driven momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar tone praised as crisp and powerful; solo on Other One highlighted.  Early vocal work described as sluggish/froggy by some reviewers.
band_member_comments.Phil: Visible smoking a doobie pre‑set; strong bass chops on Other One, Eyes and post‑Space jams noted.
band_member_comments.Bob: Distorted vocals on Women > Eyes add character; playful rap in Good Lovin' mentioned.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboard) lauded for awesome solos and solid support throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Drums: Generally described as mild‑mannered; no major criticisms or standout praise.
